mnemonics: HULK SMASH (Partial Derivatives): Hold one variable, Understand it's Like a constant, Keep differentiating with respect to the other variable
keyPoints: A function of several variables depends on more than one independent variable, e.g., f(x,y).
The partial derivative measures the rate of change of the function with respect to one variable, holding the others constant.
The gradient vector (∇f) points in the direction of the steepest ascent of the function and its magnitude is the slope in that direction.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to treat other variables as constants when taking a partial derivative.
Mistaking the gradient vector for a scalar value instead of a vector.
Assuming a function is differentiable at a point just because its partial derivatives exist there.
